entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level Space Star engine; simple SOHC design favoured by budget buyers and first-time owners.

commonFailures: Timing belt failure if not replaced at 60k miles
Coil pack failure causing misfires at higher mileage
Oil leaks from camshaft seal

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Space Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Space range spans two distinct vehicles with different reliability profiles. The Space Wagon's 4G64 petrol and 4M40 diesel engines dominate replacement enquiries due to head gasket and turbo failures, while the Space Star's 3A92 timing chain issues drive urgent searches on later models. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: 3A92 Timing Chain Stretch - Why Space Star 1.2 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain to stretch, causing rattling noise and potential valve timing loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner and guides before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; prevents catastrophic damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3A92 unit with upgraded tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when chain has already damaged valves or pistons

problems.recommendation: On a Space Star worth £1,500-£3,500, a preventative chain replacement at 50k miles is cost-effective. If rattling is already present, replacement with a rebuilt unit is usually the smarter financial decision.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3A92 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Space Star

problems.h4: 4G64 Head Gasket Failure - Why Space Wagon 2.4 Engines Overheat
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warping under thermal stress compromises head gasket seal, allowing coolant into combustion chambers.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine surface, fit new gasket and bolts
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block is undamaged and head is not cracked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4G64 unit with upgraded gasket, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when coolant contamination has damaged bearings or bores

problems.recommendation: Given the age and value of most Space Wagons, a rebuilt engine at £2,200-£2,800 often represents better long-term value than a head repair that may not address underlying block issues.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4G64 Mitsubishi Space Wagon engine replacement

problems.h4: 4M40 Turbo Actuator Failure - Why Space Wagon Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Space Wagon 2.8 TD (1994-1998, 4M40 engine code)
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo actuator diaphragm perishes, preventing boost control and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo actuator diaphragm and linkage; reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo internals are undamaged and vanes move freely

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace bearings/seals/vanes, balance and refit
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when actuator failure has caused shaft wear or vane damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4M40 unit with upgraded turbo, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option when turbo failure has caused piston or bearing damage

problems.recommendation: Actuator replacement is cost-effective if caught early. If boost loss has been prolonged, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo often delivers better reliability for the investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4M40 diesel engine prices for your Mitsubishi Space Wagon

problems.h4: 4D65/4D68 Injection Pump Failure - Why Space Wagon Diesels Won't Start
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Space Wagon 1.8/2.0 TD (1991-1998, 4D65/4D68 engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Fuel injection pump seals harden and leak, causing pressure loss and difficult cold starting.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace pump seals and O-rings; recalibrate injection timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if pump internals show no wear and leak is external

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove pump, replace worn plungers/barrels, recalibrate on test bench
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when internal wear has caused pressure loss or uneven delivery

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel unit with refurbished pump, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when pump failure has contaminated oil or damaged injectors

problems.recommendation: Seal replacement is a sensible first step on a well-maintained engine. If the pump has been running dry or leaking internally, a rebuilt engine with refurbished pump offers greater peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4D68 Mitsubishi Space Wagon diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Start-Stop System Failure - Why 2016-2017 Space Stars Won't Restart
problems.rootCause: Battery sensor or ECU communication fault prevents start-stop system from recognising battery state, disabling engine restart.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace battery sensor or perform ECU reset; test start-stop function
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for early electrical glitches with no mechanical damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full electrical diagnostic, replace faulty wiring/ECU module, reprogram
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when fault codes persist after basic reset

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine only if electrical fault has caused mechanical damage
repairOptions.longevity: Rarely needed; most start-stop issues are electrical, not mechanical

problems.recommendation: Start-stop failures are almost always electrical. Invest in proper diagnostics before considering engine replacement - most cases are resolved with sensor or ECU work under £550.
problems.cta: Find Mitsubishi Space Star electrical fault specialists near you

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Space
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing a Space Star timing chain rattle or a Space Wagon head gasket failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Space specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Space Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Space and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.descriptor: Diesel engines were exclusive to the Space Wagon MPV (1991-1998), offering strong torque for family hauling but requiring attention to injection pump and turbo maintenance.

items.title: Mitsubishi Space Petrol Engines

items.descriptor: Petrol engines span both Space Star hatchback and Space Wagon MPV, from simple SOHC units to advanced GDI technology, with timing components being the critical maintenance item.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Space Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Space production timeline (1991-2024), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Space Wagon Launch: 4G63/4G64 Petrol & 4D65 Diesel Debut
years.description: The original Space Wagon MPV launched with proven 4G-series petrol engines and the new 4D65 diesel, establishing Mitsubishi's family hauler credentials.
keyChanges: First UK sales of Mitsubishi Space Wagon MPV
4G63 2.0L and 4G64 2.4L petrol engines offered
4D65 1.8L indirect injection diesel introduced
4WD variants available on higher trims
knownFor: Robust chassis suitable for family and light commercial use
4G64 head gasket sensitivity if cooling system neglected
Simple diesel design easy for independents to maintain
High mileage examples now require budget for engine replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 1991-1993 Mitsubishi Space replacement engines

years.preview: Space Wagon Matures: 4D68 & 4M40 Diesel Options Added
years.description: Mid-life updates brought larger diesel engines, improving torque for towing and loaded use, while petrol units remained unchanged.
keyChanges: 4D68 2.0L diesel added for better torque
Flagship 4M40 2.8L diesel introduced on Executive trim
Facelifted exterior styling and interior updates
4WD remained available across diesel range
knownFor: 4M40 turbo actuator failures becoming common on high-mileage examples
4D68 injection pump seal leaks requiring attention
4G64 head gasket issues on overheated units
Strong residual appeal for 4WD diesel variants
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1998 Mitsubishi Space Wagon replacement engines
years.ticker: Space Wagon 2.4 engine replacement • 4M40 turbo failure fix • 4D68 injection pump cost

years.preview: Space Star Hatchback Launch: 4G13/4G92/4G93 Petrol Range
years.description: Mitsubishi introduced the compact Space Star hatchback, shifting focus to urban buyers with smaller, efficient petrol engines including advanced GDI technology.
keyChanges: All-new Space Star hatchback platform launched
4G13 1.3L, 4G92 1.6L and 4G93 1.8L GDI petrol engines offered
No diesel option for Space Star in UK market
Space Wagon production ended in UK after 1998
knownFor: Simple 4G13 favoured by budget buyers and first-time owners
4G93 GDI injector sensitivity requiring specialist knowledge
Timing belt service critical on all three engines
Strong used market appeal due to low running costs
years.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Space Star 1999-2005 engine prices

years.preview: Second-Gen Space Star: 3A90/3A92 Three-Cylinder Engines Debut
years.description: Mitsubishi revived the Space Star name on a new platform with modern three-cylinder engines prioritising efficiency and low emissions for urban use.
keyChanges: All-new Space Star hatchback based on Mitsubishi Mirage platform
3A90 1.0L and 3A92 1.2L three-cylinder petrol engines introduced
Start-stop system standard on most trims from 2014
CVT automatic option available on higher trims
knownFor: Excellent fuel economy and low insurance groups
Timing chain design improved but tensioner wear reported on early units
Start-stop electrical faults emerging on 2014-2016 models
Strong appeal for first-time buyers and urban commuters
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2016 Mitsubishi Space Star replacement engines

years.preview: Space Star Refinements: Start-Stop & CVT Focus
years.description: Later Space Star models received electrical system refinements and CVT calibration updates, though timing chain and start-stop issues remain key replacement drivers.
keyChanges: Start-stop system software updates for 2017+ models
CVT transmission calibration improvements
Interior and infotainment updates across trim levels
No major engine changes; 3A90/3A92 continued unchanged
knownFor: Timing chain stretch on 3A92 remains the dominant failure pattern
Start-stop battery recognition faults on 2016-2017 models largely resolved post-2017
CVT jerking complaints on early automatics improved with software updates
Strong value proposition for budget-conscious used buyers
years.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Space Star 2017-2024 engine prices

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Space Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Space engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices range from £355 for Space Star 1.3 units to £3,500 for Space Wagon 2.8 diesel, depending on engine code, condition and supplier. Supply-only prices exclude fitting labour.
keyPoints: Space Star petrol engines start from £355 rebuilt
Space Wagon petrol units range £1,800-£2,800
Diesel Space Wagon engines cost £2,200-£3,500 rebuilt
Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour depending on region
All network quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Space engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most common Mitsubishi Space engine problem?
items.answer: For Space Star models, 3A92 timing chain stretch (60-80k miles) dominates enquiries; for Space Wagon, 4G64 head gasket failure is the most frequent catastrophic issue driving replacement.
keyPoints: 3A92 timing chain rattle on cold start is early warning sign
4G64 overheating often indicates head gasket compromise
4M40 turbo actuator failure causes boost loss on diesel Wagons
Start-stop electrical faults affect 2016-2017 Space Star models
Regular servicing reduces risk but doesn't eliminate design weaknesses
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Space problem engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Mitsubishi Space?
items.answer: For Space Star models valued £1,500-£4,000, a £355-£1,400 rebuilt engine often extends life cost-effectively; for older Space Wagons, compare repair cost against vehicle value and intended use.
keyPoints: Budget £355-£1,400 for Space Star rebuilt engines
Space Wagon replacements cost £1,800-£3,500 typically
Factor in £400-£900 fitting labour if not DIY
Consider vehicle's overall condition and MOT history
A rebuilt engine with warranty can add 50k+ reliable miles
items.cta: Find out if your Mitsubishi Space is worth repairing

items.question: Which Mitsubishi Space engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The Space Star 4G13 1.3L petrol is widely regarded as the most robust, thanks to simple SOHC design and low stress; avoid neglected 4G64 petrol or high-mileage 4M40 diesel units.
keyPoints: 4G13 benefits from simple design and wide parts availability
3A92 is efficient but monitor timing chain condition
4G64 requires strict cooling system maintenance
4M40 needs specialist turbo and pump attention
Full service history is the best reliability indicator
items.cta: Compare reliable Mitsubishi Space engine options

items.question: How long does it take to replace a Mitsubishi Space engine?
items.answer: Professional fitting typically takes 1-3 working days depending on variant, with Space Star hatchbacks generally quicker than Space Wagon MPVs due to engine bay access.
keyPoints: Space Star engine swap: 1-2 days typical
Space Wagon replacement: 2-3 days due to size/access
Allow extra time for ancillary part replacement
Rebuilt engines arrive bench-tested and ready to fit
Most specialists offer loan car or collection/delivery options
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Space engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mitsubishi Space?
items.answer: While engine swaps are technically possible, we recommend like-for-like replacement using the original engine code to ensure compatibility with ECU, wiring, emissions and MOT compliance.
keyPoints: ECU and wiring loom are coded to original engine
Emissions testing requires correct engine family
Insurance and warranty may be voided by non-standard swaps
Like-for-like rebuilt units preserve vehicle value
Our network specialists can advise on borderline cases
items.cta: Get quotes for correct Mitsubishi Space engine replacement

items.question: Do Mitsubishi Space engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms apply across the UK network
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Supply & fit packages often extend warranty to installation
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Space engine warranty options

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Space Star timing chain failure?
items.answer: The 3A92 engine's timing chain tensioner can wear prematurely (60-80k miles), allowing chain stretch that causes rattling noise and, if ignored, potential valve timing loss.
keyPoints: Rattling on cold start is the key early warning sign
Regular oil changes with correct grade reduce wear risk
Replacement with upgraded tensioner resolves the issue
Ignoring symptoms can lead to catastrophic engine damage
Rebuilt 3A92 units include upgraded timing components
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Space Star 3A92 timing chain repair

items.question: How do I identify my Mitsubishi Space engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on a metal plate on the engine block (visible near the sump or cylinder head) and also appears on your V5C logbook under "Engine number".
keyPoints: Common codes: 3A92, 4G13, 4G64, 4M40, 4D68
Enter your registration on our site for instant DVLA verification
Engine code determines compatible rebuilt unit options
Take a photo of the plate when inspecting a used engine
Our specialists can help confirm code if uncertain
items.cta: Identify your Mitsubishi Space engine code instantly
